more: (part. act. of رشد) adj. Pious, faithful, orthodox, following the right path. (Plur. راشدين).

ety: v.n. fr. رشد 'to take or follow the right way'

more: p. p. رشد following the right way. 1. Guided in the right way. 2. Dutiful; filial. Farzand-i-rashīd . A favourite son.
(from رشد) part. 1. Guiding to the right way and conducting in it; God. 2. Guided to the right way and conducted in it (by God).

more: n. m. Jealousy. Rashk se lete nahīṅ nān kī sun le na koī, Dil hī dil meṅ tujhe ham yād kīyā karte haiṅ . Through jealousy your name I never take lest any hear it, But in my heart of hearts deep down I never cease to bear it.

ety: ras, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. The arriving, the attaining or obtaining; the being performed (used as last member of compounds, e.g. dād-rasī; faryād-rasī).

ex: تین لاکھ روپیہ کا جواہر ... اسی وقت دے کر رسید دستخطی لے لو
You can buy the gems ... worth three hundred thousand rupees this instance along with the signed receipt
جب یہ بخوشی رخصت کرے رسید اور صافی نامہ اس سے لیکر آویں
When he contently departs, [be sure to] bring [me] the invoice and the payment receipt from him
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رسید (rasīd, “receipt”, literally “it has arrived”). False cognate of English receipt.

ety: perf. part. of rasīdan; see ras
def: part. Arrived; at hand; received; reached or overtaken by; mature, ripe, of age (used in comp., e.g. měḥnat-rasīda, 'overtaken by trouble'; ʻumr-rasīda, 'advanced in years').
more: p. p. ( In comp .) 1. Arrived; received. 2. Matured; ripe. Umar rasīdah . Advanced in years.
(from رسيدن) part. pass. Arrived, received, ripe, overtaken by. (Plur. رسيدگان).

ety: act. part. of رسخ 'to be or become firm, &c.'

more: (part. act. of رسوخ) adj. Firm, durable, constant, rooted, sincere, learned.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Juicy, succulent, moist, liquid; under-done (meat); luscious; sensuous, voluptuous, rakish, amorous, wanton (syn. rasik).
more: adj. S. रसाल 1. Juicy; luscious; succulent. 2. Underdone (meat). 3. Voluptuous; amorous; rakish; wanton; prurient. Rasīlī chhabīlī banī tang o chust, Libās aur zevar se har ek durust . Mīr Hasan. Her mellowed rounded charms tight bound, With vesture fit and jewels crowned. rasīle bain . Poet. Sensuous or voluptuous conversation. rasīle, dal-dār . ( Bot .) Succulent; fleshy. rasīlī āṅkheṅ, rasīle nain , Liquid, sensuous, voluptuous, or wanton eyes; lascivious or amorous looks. Nek lagat mohe apne saiyāṅ kī āṅkh rasīlī lāj bharī . I love to see my husband's liquid eyes with shame suffused!

def: s.m. An epicure; one who enjoys life, or who makes pleasure his pursuit, a voluptuary, a libertine, a rake.
more: n. m. 1. A man of pleasure; a beau; a gay gallant; a lover. 2. A voluptuary; libertine; rake. 1. Taiṅ to ran kā rasiyā, ban kā basiyā, taiṅ kyā jāne chorī re; Tik tik kar-ke rain gaṅvāī, pās khaṛī thī gorī re . Play. Chaubolā. A very rustic gallant you, a woodman wight, no more, Sir, You cry 'shoo shoo' all night, the while a maid stands at your door, Sir! (The story is that a countryman, in his sleep, mistaking the knock of his mistress at his door for cattle attempting to force their way in, called out ' ṭik ṭik ,' to drive the animals away and fell asleep). 2. Nāch kā baṛā rasyā hai . 3. Chhajje kī baiṭhak burī, aur par-chhāveṅ kī chhāṅ, Dhore kā rasiyā burā jo nit ūṭh pakṛe bāṅh . Dohā. 'Tis bad to sit on open ledge or in another's shade, So is a wanton near whose hand is always on you laid.
s. m. A rake, one who enjoys life, a voluptuary.

ety: rasī = Prk. rasiʼā = S. rasikā+ā = āw = Prk. āwa or āwe = S. āpi (caus. augment)+nā

ety: Prk. रसिअडी (with w inserted) = S. रसिका+ली
def: s.f. = raswāʼī, q.v. s.f. The ceremony of distributing the first juice of the sugar-cane; the season of pressing out the juice of the sugar-cane.
n. m. S. रस quicksilver, कर्पूर camphor (from its resemblance to this drug). Corrosive sublimate, or preparation of mercury.
more: s. m. Corrosive sublimate, or a preparation of mercury similar to it, but perhaps milder; at least, if we may trust to the dose assigned by Eastern physicians, as stated in the Maḵẖzanu-l-adwiya, i. e. from 1 3/4 to 3 1/2 grains. To prepare it, quicksilver is triturated with red bole (see گيرو), marine salt, and a substance containing vitriolick acid. The Bhāva Prakāsa orders alum, the Maḵẖzanu-l-adwiya vitriol of zinc or copper; but the Sukhabodha directs sulphur: and, in the last case, the mass is to be moistened with the juice of the bark of the amrā- tree (Spondias mangifera). It is then sublimed in close earthen vessels.

def: s.f. A small pearl (a term used by jewellers).
n. f. The amorous sports of Krishnā and the gopīs or milkmaids; the religious play so called.

ety: inf. n. of رسم 'to mark, trace, delineate,' &c.; with amplification of meaning in Persian
def: s.f. Marking out, delineating, designing; — sketch, outline, model, plan; way followed (in respect of doctrine and practices of religion, &c.), manner, custom, practice, usage, settled mode; injunction, precept, canon, law; — a game among children (the laws of which are, that one puts a question to another, who is obliged to reply in words which do not contain either of the three letters س,ر, or م, which compose the word رسم: if one of these should be found in his answer, he is condemned, by way of penalty, to imitate the voice of any animal that may be directed by the questioner)
more: n. f. رسم marking. Settled mode; order ( chāl , 4.). rasm ahl-i-tijārat ; H. byohār rīt , Mercantile law; commercial usage. rasm par chalnā yā jānā , v. n. To act according to custom. rasm-i-khāndān ; H. kulā beohār , n. f. Family custom. rasm-i-zamānah , The fashion of the day; conventional usage. rasm-i-mulk ; H. desā chāl , n. f. The custom of the country. rasm o rivāj . Custom and usage; established practice. bāp dādā yā baṛoṅ kī rasm, purānī yā qadīm rasm . An old custom. mazhabī rasm ; H. dharm-rīt . Religious ceremony.

(from رسم rasama, Heb. He marked or wrote) s. f. 1. Custom. 2. Law. 3. Model, plan. 4. A game among children, the laws of which are, that one puts a question to another, who is obliged to reply in words which do not contain either of the three letters س ر or م which compose the word رسم. If one of these should be found in his answer, he is condemned, by way of penalty, to imitate the voice of any animal which may be directed by the questioner.
subs: To act according to custom, to follow the custom or practice (of, -kī)
To be marked out, be delineated, be sketched out; to become a custom or practice, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رسم (rasm), from Arabic رَسْم (rasm).

adj. Usual, customary; — middling, second sort or rate, ordinary.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رسمی (rasmī), from Arabic رَسْمِيّ (rasmiyy). Compare Punjabi رسمی / ਰਸਮੀ (rasmī).

more: adj. 1. Common ( chaltā , 5.). 2. Middling; second-rate. rasmiyāt-i-byāh ; H. byāh-rīt , Marriage ceremonies. rasmiyāt-i-maiyat ; H. kiryā karam . Funeral ceremonies; obsequies.
adj. 1. Customary. 2. Middling, second sort, ordinary.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رسمی (rasmī), from Arabic رَسْمِيّ (rasmiyy). Compare Punjabi رسمی / ਰਸਮੀ (rasmī).

def: n. f. S. रसवती. 1. Victuals; food. 1. Rasoī aur rasāīn barābar . Prov. The culinary art and the alchemist's are both difficult. 2. Dījo more bābal usī ghar jahāṅ bāmnī kareṅ rasoī! Dījo more bābul usī ghar jahāṅ hoṅ bāṅdiyān! Wom. song. A house my father give, with a Brahman cook to dress my food, A house my father give with slave girls waiting on each mood! 3. Mās binā sab ghās rasoī . Prov. Without some meat for zest the meal's but grass at best. 4. Khāṅṛ binā sab rāṅḍ rasoī . Prov. No sugar in your mess it's quite tasteless. 2. A place for cooking; a kitchen. 3. The Hindu's dining room and kitchen combined. rasoī banānā, yā k. , v. a. To cook; dress food. rasoī jīmnā, khānā, yā pānā , v. n. adj. 1. Right, true, good, just, upright, honest, sincere. 2. Right (opposite to left). 3. Straight, even, level. 4. adv. Actually, certainly. راست آنا rāst ānā, v. n. To regain oneʼs temper, to be set to rights. راست باز rāst-bāz, adj. Honest, upright, righteous, faithful, to be depended on, candid, sincere. راست بازي rāst-bāz-ī, s. f. ety: perf. part. of rastan; rt. Zend raz = S. रह्
def: part. Delivered, liberated, saved; escaped.
adj. Dexterous, right-handed; — s.m. A road, way, &c. (see rāstā).
more: n. m. Z. rās . 1. A way; road; street; lane; alley. 1. Kyā rastā nāpne āë the ? You must be measuring the road. (No sooner come than gone!). 2. Apne raste ānā, apne raste jānā . Come your own way, go your own way. (Dont interfere with any body). 3. Kyā terī partīt, musāfir, mat nā kare averī! Chhoṛ de rastā, jān de mujh ko, sās laṛegī merī! Play. How can I trust thee, stranger; dont delay me, pray! My mother-in-law will chide me; let me go my way! 2. Manner ( ḍhaṅg , 1.). rāsta batānā , v. a. To direct one on his way; shew the way; guide. rāsta-chaltā . A wayfarer; traveller. Rasta-chattoṅ se laṛtī hai . She fights with every wayfarer (with every body). rāsta dekhnā , v. a. To wait for one ( intizār k .). rāsta roknā , v. a. To stop one's way. rāsta katrānā , v. a. To take another way; dodge or turn aside. rāste par ānā , v. a. To come on the right road; mend one's way. apnā rāsta lo! rā…
1. adj. Right handed. 2. s. m. Road: see راستا rāstā.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian راسته (rāsta). Compare Bengali রাস্তা (rasta), Gujarati રસ્તો (rasto), Kannada ರಸ್ತೆ (raste), Marathi रस्ता (rastā), Punjabi ਰਸਤਾ (rastā).

def: s.f.
adj. Juicy, tasty, savoury, palatable; — s.m. Blood.
s.f. Night
more: n. f. S. रात्रि, Pr. ratti, rāī . Sant. nindā . Night; nightfall. 1. Rāt mā kā pet hai . Wom. Prov. The night is as the mother's womb. (It covers all things). 2. Rāt kī nīyat harām . Prov. The vow of the night is unblessed. 3. Rāt Narbadā utrī, subah uṭh kueṅ se ḍarī . Prov. The Narbada she crossed at night, next morning at a well took fright. (She was afraid to draw water from the well). 4. Rāt aṅdherī māṅ mat soiyo! Pahroṅ nāth chau-māse māṅ! Rus. Wom. Dont, wifey, with your nose-ring sleep! Rain-nights are dark, thieves lurking keep. 5. Rāt baṭyā chāl na, sāthan rī! Rāt bāṭ jo tauṅh chalegī lut jāgā terā dhan rī! Rus. Wom. song. Dont walk out in the night, my dear! If in the night out you appear Your jewels (beauty) will be rifled, dear! 6. Sāīṅ jī! tūṅ dīyā, ham bātī, Jaise sāf jarelā sārī rātī . Bhoj. Wom. song. I am the wick, and you, my spouse, the light That burneth all so bright…

s. f. Night. رات تهوڙي اورسانگ بـﮩت rāt thoṛī aur sāng bahut, is applied to express a hurry of business with little time to perform it in; also great expense with a small income. Mīr says:-- بن جو کچهـ بن سکي جواني مين رات تهوڙي هي اوربـﮩت هي سانگ Ban jo kuchh ban sake jawānī meṇ; rāt thoṛī hai aur bahut hai sāng, Acquire whatever you can in the season of youth: the task is arduous and the time short. (Ars longa vita brevis. Hippoc. ). آدهي رات ādhī rāt, Midnight. راتون رات rātoṅ rāt, In the middle of the night.
subs: adv. After staying a night or for the night
adv. The whole night, all night

'The night is short and the business great (or the play long)'; much to do and little time to do it in, or the task is arduous and the time short; (cf. Ars longa vita brevis, and 'Art is long and time is fleeting')
s.m. lit. 'Night-wanderer'; a goblin, evil spirit; a thief

def: part. & s.m. Pleased, delighted (with), satisfied; enamoured (of), fondly attached (to), taking pleasure (in); inclined (to), disposed; loved, beloved; intent (on, -meṅ), addicted (to); devoted (to), occupied (in), engaged (in); — pleasure, enjoyment, enjoyment of love, sexual union, coition, copulation; the private parts
more: n. f. S. रुत ऋतु Pr. udo . 1. Season, Weather. The year is divided into six seasons, viz. Basan spring, Grīshmā (H. garmī ) hot weather, Varshā (H. barsāt ) rains, Sharad autumn, Him (H. jāṛā ) cold, and Shishira dewy or foggy. 2. Menstruation ( kapṛe ). 3. The rutting season; a stag in rut. rut asnān . Bathing after menstruation. rut badal , n. f. Change of season. rut badal kī bīmārī , Illness caused by change of season. rut badalnā, palaṭnā, yā phir jānā , v. n. To be changed (the season). be-rut-kā , adj. Unseasonable; out of season; early or late.
s. f. Season, weather. The Hindūs divide the year into six seasons; viz. वसंत vasanta, ग्रीष्म grīsh̤ma, वर्षा varsh̤ā, शरद् sarad, हिम hima, and शिशिर sisira.
s. f. 1. Love. 2. Venery, coition. 3. Name of the wife of Kāmadeva. See رتي ratī.

def: n. m. S. रथ from ऋ to go. 1. A native chariot; a car; a covered carriage used chiefly by women; the car in which idols are conveyed. Hirnā bhale ki Har bhale, shugnā bhale ki Shyām? Arjun rath ko hāṅk do, bhalī kareṅge Rām! A deer prefer to Har, an omen heed or Shyām? Drive on the chariot Arjun, good will come from Rām! 2. ( Chess ) The castle. rath-jāttrā , n. f. A procession of chariots or of the car of any Hindu god, esp. that of Jagan nāth on the 2nd of the light fortnight of Āsāṛh . rath-vān, rath-bān , n. m. One who drives a rath. rath-vānī, rath-bānī , n. f. Coachmanship. rath'ī , n. m. 1. A rider in a car. 2. A minister.

more: n. f. S. रति from रम to enjoy. 1. Enjoyment; pleasure. 2. The sexual passion; sexual intercourse. 3. The Venus of the Hindūs, and the wife of Kām-deo (Cupid). 4. Propitious star; fortune; luck. Āj kal ratī sāmne hai. ratī chamaknā, yā jāgnā , v. n. To begin to prosper, or thrive. Āp se ātī hūṅ terī pās maiṅ bhāgī hūī. In dinoṅ rattī du-gānā hai terī jāgī huī . Wom. Rangīn. Of mine own self I run and come to you, My bedfellow! good fortune smiles on you.

1. ( रति ) n. prop. The Venus of the Hindūs, and the wife of Kāmadeva (Cupid), who was consumed by the fiery rage of Mahādeva for interrupting him in his devotions; and, Rati, being disconsolate for the loss of her husband, was informed by Pārvatī that he would be born in the house of Kr̤ish̤ṇa, and would have the name of Pradyumna; but, that Rājā Sambara would steal him away and cast him into the sea; that, thence, he would be taken in the belly of a fish to the kitchen of Sambara; and, that she must go and wait for him there. Following this advice, she remained in the kitchen of that Rājā, till it happened that a large fish, which had been presented to him, on being opened by the cook, was found to contain another fish; and, when this was opened, a child issued from its belly. This child the cook gave to Rati: and the matter being reported to the Rājā, he directed her to take care of t…
subs: s.m. 'Ratī's husband,' Kām-deva, the god of love
(or rattī) jāgnā or ćamaknā, v.n. To begin to prosper, to thrive, flourish
s.m. Sexual intercourse, the enjoyment of carnal love

Clarified butter ( ghī ). rau/?/gan-i-qāz malnā , v. n. To butter a person; to beslobber; slaver. Ajī! maiṅ aisā raugan-i-qāz malūṅ ki dushman bhī apnā ho jāë . Why Sir! s.m. A vessel for boiling butter in

ety: Prk. रमणअं = S. रमणीयं, rt. रम्
def: s.m. The bringing home a wife to consummate a marriage (it is a ceremony intermediate between the marriage and the gaunā, q.v.).
more: n. m. S. रमण. 1. A noose ( phāṅsā ). 2. Bringing home a wife to consummate a marriage. It is an intermediate ceremony between the marriage and the gaunā , q. v. In some places the two meanings are inverted. 3. Small shot or pebbles inserted to produce a tinkling sound.
s. m. Bringing home a wife to consummate a marriage.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit रोवइ (rovaï), रोअइ (roaï), रोयइ (royaï), from Sanskrit रोदति (rodati), रोदिति (róditi).

def: conj. 'to render clear'), s.f. Lustre, water (of a sword, &c.); brightness, splendour, beauty, elegance, grace, ornament; freshness, prime; colour, complexion; flourishing state or condition; — (met.) order, symmetry
more: n. f. رنق cleared. See rūp , (2). 1. Complexion; color. 1. Uske chehre par raunaq hai . 2. Sārī raunaq chār paise kī hai . For two pence you 've this painted show. 2. Splendour; dignity. 3. ( Illit. Fac .) Tumult; uproar; row. raunaq-afroz h. , v. n. 1. To grace or honor by one's presence; confer dignity on. 2. To arrive at a place; visit a person. raunaq-dār , adj. Brilliant; splendid.

(from رنق) s. f. 1. Beauty, elegance, splendour, ornament, gayety. 2. ( met. ) Order, symmetry. رونق افزاهونا raunaḳ-afzā-honā, v. n. To grace (by the presence of the person addressed or spoken of, when a person of consequence honours a place with his presence); to arrive. رونق دار raunaḳ-dār, adj. Brilliant, splendid.
subs: raunaq-afroz honā, v.n. To grace or honour (by one's arrival or presence), to confer dignity (on); to arrive
To be in full bloom or splendour, to be in (its) prime; to be in a flourishing state

is śahar mẽ koī raunaq nahī̃ hai.
There is no splendour in this city.
وہ ہر محفل کی رونق ہے۔
voh har mahfil kī raunaq hai.
They are the life of every party.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رَوْنَق (rawnaq), borrowed from Arabic رَوْنَق (rawnaq). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi رونق (rvnq /⁠raunaq⁠/). Cognate with Punjabi رَوݨَق (rauṇaq).

def: one who enlightens, an illuminator; a spiritual teacher, guide, or leader; God, the illuminator of all, He who created and shone His divine creative light upon all of existence

ety: Pehl. roshan; Zend raokhshna, rt. rukhsh; S. रोचन, rt. रुच्
def: adj. Light, lighted up, alight, illuminated; bright, shining, splendid, luminous; clear, evident, manifest, conspicuous

adj. Of luminous mind, of splendid genius
v.t. To light (a lamp, candle, &c.); to illumine, enlighten; to bring to light, make manifest; to clear (from ambiguity)
s.m. One who makes bright or light, an illuminator; a polisher, furbisher
To be lighted, become illuminated, become bright; to be or become clear or evident (to, -par).

part. act. (of رفتن To go) In compos. Going; as, پيشرو pesh-rau, Preceding, going or singing before another. رودينا rau denā, To copulate (used among women).

def: part. adj. Going; flowing; passing; current; lawful, right; equitable, reasonable; admissible, allowable; approved, tolerated; worthy of reception or attention; worthy, proper, right, suitable (syn. jāʼiz)

adj. 1. Right. See جاپز Rau-meṅ sab ravā hai . Prov. Whatever comes with the stream is lawful prize. 2. Allowable; current. 3. Approved; worthy of reception or attention. ravā jān'nā, yā samajhnā , v. n. To think fit or proper; to approve. ravā-dār , adj. Approving; judging or holding to be right. ravā rakh' nā , v. a. 1. To receive; acquiesce in; countenance. 2. To uphold; maintain; justify 3. To warrant; make legal ( jāiz rakhnā ).

1. part. pres. (from رفتن) Going, passing. 2. adj. Allowable, lawful, current, right, proper, accurate, worthy, obtained. روا دار rawā-dār, An approver, chooser, consenter, approving, judging, or holding (it) right. روا داري rawā-dār-ī, s. f. s. m. 1. Gold or silver filings. 2. A grain or particle of sand, dust, gunpowder, &c.

v.t. To hold, esteem, or consider lawful, right, or proper; to uphold, maintain, justify: to receive, acquiesce in; — to make current, or legal, &c.; to warrant
v.t. To make going; to cause to flow; to supply, render obtainable.

Public policy; custom of the country. rivāj mulkī yā dehātī , n. Local usage or observance. rivāj hai . Is usual.
(corrupt. riwāj, from روج) 1. s. m. Usage, custom, fashion, currency, vent, sale. 2. adj. Customary, current, vendible. رواج دينا rawāj-denā, To publish, to make current or customary.
subs: v.n. To obtain currency, pass current; to be customary; to become prevalent; to be in force; to be in fashion; to have effect
v.n. To become current or customary, to prevail

def: s.m. lit. 'The Vociferator'; name of the ruler of Lankā or Ceylon, and the famous chief of the Rākshasas or demons whose subjugation and destruction by Rāmćandra, the seventh incarnation of Vishṇu, form the subject of the epic poem called Rāmāyaṇ, (he was the son of Viśravas by Kaikasī, and the half-brother of Kuvera).
more: n. m. King of Lankā or Ceylon who carried away Sītā , and lost his life and kingdom to her husband Rāmchandra. Rāvan kī sanyā, lit . Rāvan's army. Black men in black clothes.
s. m. Son of Visravā and Kaikasī, and sovereign of Lankā (Ceylon), who carried way Sītā, and lost his life and kingdom to her husband Rāmachandra, q. v.

ety: Prk. रमणो, or रवँणु?; S. रमणः
def: s.m. Husband; lover, sweetheart, gallant; — raun, s.m. = raunā, q.v. s.m. The bringing home a wife to consummate a marriage (it is a ceremony intermediate between the marriage and the gaunā, q.v.).
more: s. m. A husband, lover, a gallant. ¶ rūṅ ( रोम ) s. f. (dakh.) Down, small hair of the body: see روم rom.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa रवण (ravaṇa), from Prakrit 𑀭𑀫𑀡 (ramaṇa), from Sanskrit रमण (rámaṇa). Cognate with Gujarati રમણ (ramaṇ).

ety: prop. ruwān; old P. ruãn, rvãn; Pehl. rubān; Zend urvan
def: s.m. The rational soul; soul, spirit, life.
wety: From Classical Persian روان (ravān, “fluid, flowing”), a compound of رو (rav) + ـان (-ân); the former is the present stem of رفتن (raftan).

def: adj. Going, passing, moving, running, current; flowing, fluid, fluent, running or flowing smoothly; quick, brisk, active, sharp, expert, dexterous; — s.m. Text; reading; — adv. Fluently, smoothly; quickly, briskly, &c.
more: (part. pres. of رفتن) 1. adj. Going, moving, flowing, running, fluent, current. 2. s. m. Text. 3. Reading. 4. Life, soul. روان کرنا rawāṅ karnā, v. a. 1. To set agoing. 2. To despatch, to forward, to send. 3. To make current.
subs: v.n. To go running about, to wander to and fro, to stroll up and down
v.t. To set agoing; to make current; to cause to flow, to spill, shed; to supply ( = rawā karnā); to send, despatch, forward; to impart briskness to, make active or quick; to sharpen, whet.
wety: From Classical Persian روان (ravān, “fluid, flowing”), a compound of رو (rav) + ـان (-ân); the former is the present stem of رفتن (raftan).

def: adj. & s.m. = روانه rawāna, q.v.
more: (caus. of رونا ronā, q. v.) v. a. To make cry, to vex, to displease.

def: adj. Going, &c. ( = rawān); departed; — despatched, sent; — s.m. A custom-house passport, a pass, permit
more: 1. s. m. A pass, passport. 2. adj. Despatched, departed, proceeding. روانه کرنا rawāna karnā, To despatch. روانه هونا rawāna honā, 1. To flow. 2. To be despatched, to be in motion, to go, to pass, to proceed.
subs: v.n. To be flowing, to flow; to be in motion, be proceeding; to proceed; to depart, to set out, to start (on a journey); to depart this life, to die; to be despatched, be sent.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian روانه (rawāna), from رو (raw), the present stem of رفتن (raftan, “to go, leave”). Compare Punjabi ਰਵਾਨਾ (ravānā) / روانہ (ravānā), Bengali রওয়ানা (roōẇana), Marathi रवाना (ravānā).

def: s.f. Running, flowing, flux; going, passing; travelling, setting out, departure; despatch, forwarding (of a letter, &c.); — purport of a pass, or passport, &c.
more: n. f. 1. Setting out; departure; embarkation. 2. Despatch (of a letter, etc). 3. The purport of a permit, pass, or authority to proceed. Koī to lambī ḍāṛhī līe ho gayā ravāṅ, Mūchheṅ, bh/?/veṅ talak koī muṅḍvā-ke mar gayā . On death. Nazīr. And with long beards some met their death and took their way, Moustache and eyebrows shaven some hence passed away. ravāngī chiṭṭhī , 1. A pass; passport. 2. Port clearance. ravāngī-i-māl . Despatch of goods; consignment.
s. f. 1. Running, flowing. 2. Passing, travelling, going, departure.
subs: s.f. A pass, passport; port clearance.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رَوَانَگِی (rawānagī).

def: v. a. To send; despatch (a letter, messenger, etc.). ravānah h. , v. n. 1. To proceed; set out; depart. 2. To be sent or despatched. 3. To go to the next world; to die.
s.m. = ramaṇak, q.v. s.m. Name of a Varsha; name of the regent of this Varsha; name of a Dvīp.
s.m. A filter made of cane or bamboo.

s.f. corr. of raunaq, q.v. conj. 'to render clear'), s.f. Lustre, water (of a sword, &c.); brightness, splendour, beauty, elegance, grace, ornament; freshness, prime; colour, complexion; flourishing state or condition; — (met.) order, symmetry
subs: raunaq-afroz honā, v.n. To grace or honour (by one's arrival or presence), to confer dignity (on); to arrive
To be in full bloom or splendour, to be in (its) prime; to be in a flourishing state

def: s.f. Rhubarb (root of the plant called rebās, q.v.)
more: s. Rhubarb. راوندچيني rāwand-i-chīnī, Chinese rhubarb. See ريوند rewand.

def: Rwanda (a country in East Africa)
wety: Borrowed from English Rwanda.

ety: raw, q.v.+Zend aff. añṭ = S. अन्त्
def: part. (of raftan) & s.m. Going, travelling; — goer, traveller, passenger.
s.m. Name of Indra-jit, the eldest son of the demon Rāvan.
s.f. Going, proceeding; course; running; currency; flowing, flow, flux; effusion; fluency; reading; briskness (of market).
more: s. f. Going, proceeding, running, flowing; fluency, reading.

def: s.f. = ramaṇī, q.v. s.f. A beautiful young woman, a charming woman; a mistress; a wife; — mons veneris; a species of Sanskrit metre (four times ̆̆¯, or four times ̆̆¯̆̆¯).
more: Tir n. f. A woman ( tiryā ).

def: blood circulation, flow of blood
wety: From روانی (ravānī, “fluid, flowing”) + ئ (-e, “ezafe”) + خون (xūn, “blood”), literally meaning "flow of blood", either borrowed through Classical Persian or a Persianized Urdu-borne formation.

def: s.m. = رونه rawanna, q.v.
more: s. m. A servant who attends at the gate of the womenʼs apartments to purchase articles that are required.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit रोवइ (rovaï), रोअइ (roaï), रोयइ (royaï), from Sanskrit रोदति (rodati), रोदिति (róditi).

ety: corr. of P. rawanda, or rawāna
def: s.m. A pass, permit, a passport; — a servant who attends at the gate of the women's apartments to purchase articles that are required; an errand-boy.
more: n. m. Cor. of ravānah , from رفتن to go. 1. A passport. 2. Mah. Wom. A servant who attends at the gate of the women's apartments to purchase articles that are required; an errand boy; a page. 1. Ek mere ghar annā, dūsre ravannā . Prov. A servant girl I keep and eke an errand boy! (How rich I am!) 2. Tabarruk hai merā purānā ravannā, Ye sab hai ravannoṅ kā nānā ravannā! Wom. Rangīn. A sacred relic is my ancient errand boy! The grandsire of all errand boys my errand boy!
(from روانه) s. m. A pass, a permit.

ety: S. रोमशः; or rawāṅ = roʼāṅ, 'hair'+s = sā; cf. roʼāṅsā below
def: s.m. A kind of bean, Dolichos sinensis.
more: n. m. A kind of bean; Dolichos sinensis . Wat.
s. m. A kind of bean. (Dolichos sinensis).

def: adj. = roʼāsā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Inclined to weep, ready to cry, lachrymose, tearful; putting on a crying face, pouting.

r: rāwar, rāʼur, rāʼorā, rāʼurā, rāʼoro, rāʼorau
def: pron. (f. -ī), Of you, yours ( = bumhārā, q.v.).
s. f. Going, travelling.
s. f. Going, travelling.
s.f. See s.v. raw, 'going.' s.m. Cry, clamour, din; sound, noise; voice; hum; report, fame.
more: n. f. 1. Moving; travelling. 2. Haste; hurry ( chalā-chalī ). Ravāravī meṅ asbāb na bāṅdh sakā .

def: s.f. (dialec.) = rawāṅs, q.v. s.m. A kind of bean, Dolichos sinensis.
adj. (f. ravantī), Crying out, raising a cry, bellowing.
more: n. S. रोटिक. 1. A large loaf. Sab se bhalā bhīk kā roṭ . Prov. No loaf like the beggar's loaf. (It is got without labour). 2. A sweet cake offered to a god.

def: s.m. Prince; chieftain, chief; hero, warrior; — a high caste of sweepers (who do not eat the leavings of others, as sweepers commonly do); an individual of that caste (cf. měh-tar).
more: n. m. 1. See rāval . 2. A high sweeper caste who dont eat the leavings of others, as sweepers commonly do.
s. m. 1. A hero. 2. A sweeper (of a particular cast).

def: s.m. (in Bundelkhanḍ), Bread made of kodoṅ or sāṅwāṅ.
s.m. The Indian cuckoo, the Koʼil.
s.m. pl. (of rātib, q.v.), Necessaries of life, &c.
s.m. The sun
more: n. m. ऋ to go. The sun. Bidhu mahi pūr piyūshanh, ravi tap jitneu kāj, Māṅge bārid dehiṅ jal Rāmchadr ke rāj. Rāmāyan. ravi-kānt , n. m. 1. The sun's rays. 2. A crystal. ravi-mārg , n. m. The sun's path. ravi-manḍal , n. m. The disc of the sun. ravi-vār , n. m. Sunday ( itvār ).
s. f. Cotton. روي دار rūʼī-dār, adj. Quilted with cotton, stuffed with cotton.
subs: s.f. 'Daughter of the sun' ( = ravi-nanḍinī, q.v.)
s.m. A sort of crystal, sun-stone; — ray of the sun
s.m. Name of an old Hindū commentator
s.f. 'Daughter of the sun,' an epithet of the river Jamnā — ravi-vār, vulg. rabi-bār, s.m. Sunday (syn. itwār).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit रवि (ravi).

ety: for A. راوٍ rāwiṉ, act. part. of روي 'to bring water'; 'to transmit orally,' &c.
def: s.m. Relater, reciter, rehearser, narrator, historian; author of a fable, romance, or fiction, &c. (Pers. pl. rāwiyān; Ar. pl. (obl.) rāwiyīn, Relaters, &c.).
more: n. m. روي related. A narrator; historian. Darog ba-gardan-i-rāvī . Prov. For aught that's false the narrator is to blame.
(part. act. of روي) s. m. An historian, the author of a fable, romance, fiction, &c. narrator, relater. (Plur. راويين).

ety: rav, q.v.+aff. ish = Zend shi or she
def: s.f. Motion, walk, gait, carriage; practice, custom, fashion, usage; rule, institution, law; conduct, behaviour; order, course, proceeding, procedure; manner, method, mode, way; — a garden-walk, path, avenue, passage, gallery.
more: n. f. رو go. See چال 1 to 7. (Pop. raush ). A foot path in a garden; an avenue.
(from رفتن) s. f. 1. Custom, fashion, institution, law, rule. 2. Avenue, walk, passage, way.

def: Sunday
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit रविवार (ravivāra).

def: s.m. Custom, practice, fashion, way, manner, behaviour, conduct; rule, law, institution (syn. ćalan).
more: n. m. رﮩـا go. See chalan , 1, 2.
(from رفتن) s. m. Custom, fashion, manner, institution, law, rule.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رویه (rawiya), from رفتن (raftan, “to go”).

ety: raw˚ = Prk. रव(इ), fr. S. रव, rt. रु; or S. रौ(ति), rt. रु
def: v.n. To give forth sound, to sound, to make a noise; — s.m. A thing that gives forth sound (as a rattle, &c.); small pebbles or shot, &c. inserted in anything to produce a sound or noise.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit रोवइ (rovaï), रोअइ (roaï), रोयइ (royaï), from Sanskrit रोदति (rodati), रोदिति (róditi).

def: s.m. A cloth spread upon a bed, &c. (for a person to lie upon).
s.m. Stream (of a river), current; rapid flow, velocity, speed, swiftness.
more: f. ( रे ) A vocative particle used by way of disrespect or admiration generally; holla! bravo! O!

ety: for رأيٌ, inf. n. of رأي 'to see; to suppose,' &c.
def: s.f. Belief, judgment, opinion, notion, thought, meditation, deliberation; wisdom, intelligence, skill in affairs; counsel, advice
subs: (-kī), To ask the opinion (of), seek the advice (of), to consult
(-kī), To accept the opinion (of), to submit or bow to the judgment (of)
To give an opinion; to vote
s.m. Expression of opinion; a communication.

def: s.m. Prince; chief; a Hindū title (cf. rāʼo); (in comp. as first member) any principal object, anything the best or largest of its kind
subs: s.f. A species of jasmine, Jasminum zambac
s.f. A species of the jamblong tree, Calyptranthes caryophyllifolia
s.m. 'Prince of princes,' a title among Hindūs; the Dīwān of the Khālisa or chief treasurer
s.m. A prince's son, a young prince
(or munī), rāʼe-maniyā, s.m. A kind of rice (in the husk)
s.m. The lāl or male bird of the Fringilla amandava (see muniyā).

def: s.m. = rājā, and rāy, qq.v. s.m. (used in comp.) King, prince, sovereign, chief, master; any principal object, anything the best, or the largest, of its kind; — a master-mason, mason, builder, bricklayer
more: n. m. A species of mustard with large seeds.
s. m. Chaff of wheat, bran.
s. m. A species of mustard with large seeds.
subs: (˚ja+abh˚), vulg. rāj-abhishek, s.m. Consecration or coronation of a king, royal inauguration or installation (syn. rāj-tilak); — rājājnā, vulg. rājāggyā (˚ja+āj˚), s.f. King's edict, royal decree or command, mandate, ordinance
vulg. rāj-adhirāj (˚ja+adh˚), s.m. King of kings, supreme ruler of princes, paramount sovereign, emperor, mighty potentate
vulg. rāj-adhikār (˚ja+adh˚), s.m. Paramount power; royal prerogative
vulg. rāj-adhikārī, s.m. (f. -inī), 'Royal official'; one who holds sway, regent, governor, ruler, king
(˚ja+āv˚), s.m. A kind of diamond or other gem ( = rāj-paṭṭ, q.v.)
vulg. rājāʼolī (˚ja+āv˚), s.f. Line of kings, royal dynasty or genealogy, royal house ( = rāj-vaṅś or rāj-baṅs)
vulg. raj-bahā, s.m. Principal channel, main distributory, the principal or common branch (of a canal, &c.)
s.m. Royal storehouse; public warehouse; royal exchequer, public treasury; royal kitchen
s.m. Food placed before an idol at noon; endowment of a temple
s.f. A present made by an inferior on admission to the presence of a great man; — fee paid to a public functionary for permission to reap the harvest
s.m. Royal cushion or throne ( = rāj-paṭṭ; see also s.v. rāj, 'government')
vulg. rāj-pat, s.m. 'Lord of kings,' prince (a Hindū title)
s.f. King's wife, royal consort, queen
s.m. King's highway, public road, main road, principal street
s.m. Royal fillet, a tiara; royal cushion, throne; a kind of precious stone, a diamond of inferior quality (said to be brought from Vīrāṭ-deś, in the north-west of India)
s.m. One who accedes to the position and office of king
s.m. and rāj-purī, s.f. = rāj-dhānī, q.v.
s.m. Royal power and splendour, kingly dignity, majesty
s.m. Royal government; royal or public policy; administration

(S. rāja-putra), s.m. (f. -nī), lit. 'King's son or descendant'; name of a military tribe of Hindūs (of the second or Kshatriya caste); name of a class of Hindūs who claim descent from the ancient Kshatriyas; — a member of the Rājpūt caste, a Rājpūt
adj. Like, or belonging to, the Rājpūts; — prop. n. A name for the province of Ajmīr, the country of the Rājpūts
s.f. The essential quality of a Rājpūt, courage, prowess in war
s.m. The king's evil (?); a kind of carbuncle or large boil (which generally rises at the back of the neck and commonly proves fatal)
s.m. The mark placed on the forehead of a royal personage on the occasion of coronation; coronation, installation (cf. rājābhishek); a mark put on the forehead of a royal personage by his vassals
s.m. Royal splendour or dignity, majesty
s.m. The discus of Krishna; — fear or perplexity occasioned by a monarch
adj. (f. -ā), Given or allowed by the king or by Government
s.f. An allowance of land by the king or by Government
s.m. The act of seeing, or appearing before, a king, a royal audience, royal levée
s.f. The royal hall of audience, presence chamber
s.m. 'The act of injuring a king.' high treason, rebellion; — tyranny
adj. & s.m. Rebellious, disloyal; — ʻking-injurer,' traitor, rebel
s.m. King's sceptre, royal sceptre; kingly authority; punishment inflicted by order of a king; tax or fine payable to royalty
(-danḍa+apa˚), s.m. 'Seizure of property in execution of a royal or of a legal decree,' confiscation
s.m. The king's gate, gate of a royal palace, palace-gate, royal entrance
s.f. King's residence, a palace; royal city, seat of government, capital, metropolis
'The king's yoke,' the burden of government, kingly responsibility, royal functions
vulg. rāj-dharam, s.m. A king's duty; the duties of a sovereign, the functions or obligations of government; — duties of the military caste
s.m. Royal revenue or right to property; public property
s.f. Queen-consort, royal spouse; — an epithet of the goddess Kālī
(rāja+r̤ishi), s.m. A royal Rishi or saint; Rishi of royal descent; that holy and superhuman personage which a king or man of the military class may become by the performance of great austerities
s.m. A mortal disease; consumption; (fig.) any great affliction or evil (as a long-pending law-suit, &c.); a mania of any kind (as for building, &c.)
s.f. A royal assembly or court; an assemblage of princes; an assembly in which the king is present, royal council; king's privy council; parliament
vulg. rāj-asthān, s.m. King's palace, &c. ( = rāj-mandir, or rāj-dhānī); — prop. n. Another name for the province of Rājpūtāna
vulg. rāj-sū, s.m. A great sacrifice or religious ceremony performed at the coronation of a supreme sovereign or universal monarch by the king himself and his tributary princes

s.m. Attendant on a royal personage, a king's servant; a courtier
s.m. Royal edict, decree, mandate; king's sway
rāj-kārva, s.m. King's business, royal duties, state affairs, functions of government, public administration

s.m. King's family, royal family, royal race or dynasty; king's court; court of justice; — (f. -ā), a king's son, a prince
vulg. rāj-kumbar, rāj-kuṅʼar, rāj-kuṅwar, rāj-kuwar, s.m. A king's son, a prince

rāj-gaddī, s.f. Royal cushion or seat, king's throne, royal throne ( = rāj-siṅghāsan)

s.m. King's house, royal dwelling, palace
s.m. A species of flat fresh-water fish
rāja-gha, rāja-ghna, s.m. King-killer, a regicide

s.f. Good fortune or glory of a king; — a royal welcome
vulg. rāj-laććhan, s.m. Royal sign or token, any mark (on the body, &c.) indicating a future king; marks of royalty; royal insignia, regalia
s.f. Royal residence, palace, royal apartment
s.f. Royal signet or seal, privy seal
s.m. Minister or counsellor of state, prime-minister, chancellor, privy counsellor
s.f. Senate, cabinet council, privy council; — rāj-mandir, rāj-mandil, s.m. Palace of a king, royal mansion or residence
s.m. = rāj-nīti or nīt, s.f. Royal conduct or policy, administration of government, regal polity, princely duties; science of government; statesmanship; politics; — code of law, jurisprudence
s.m. Country of rājās, royal domains; a kingdom (cf. rajwāṛā)
adj. & adv. Like a king, kingly, royal, princely
s.m. Family of kings, royal race or family, dynasty
(f. -inī), rāj-vaṅśīya, (f. -ā), rāj-vaṅśya (f. -ā), adj. & s.m. Belonging to a royal family, of regal race or descent; — offspring of royalty; a tribe of Rājpūts
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Of royal seed, sprung from a royal race; a royal personage

adj. Befitting a king, suitable for royalty; kingly, princely, royal.

def: s.m. Wine; — afternoon.

r: rayā, rāy, rāʼi, rāyā, rahā, rahnā, raynā
def: 1˚ = rāy or rāʼi; 2˚ = rāyā; 3˚ = rahā, perf. part. of rahnā (cf. raynā).
s.m. pl. (of raiḥān), Sweet-smelling plants, odoriferous herbs.
more: s. plur. of ريحان rīḥān, q. v.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = rain, q.v
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi رین (ryn) (c. 1611), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀭𑀬𑀡𑀻 (rayaṇī), from Sanskrit रजनी (rajanī́), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *Hráȷ́aniH, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *HráǰaniH, from Proto-Indo-European *h₁régʷenih₂, from *h₁régʷos (“darkness”).

ety: for A. راية, v.n. fr. رأي 'to see'
def: s.f. Banner, standard (of an army).
more: (from رأي Heb. To see; because it is seen by all or is conspicuous) s. f. A standard.

def: s.f. pl. (of rāyat, q.v.), Banners, standards (esp. of an army in motion); (met.) a camp.
more: s. plur. (of رايت) 1. Standards, banners (especially of an army in motion). 2. ( met. ) A camp.

def: v.n. corr. of rahnā, q.v. v.t. To roughen a mill-stone (by digging little pits in its surface with a small pickaxe).

ety: Pehl. rāj; Zend razaṅh, rt. raz; S. रहस्, rt. रह्
def: s.m. A secret, mystery
more: n. m. A secret ( bhed , 2.). rāz-dār , n. m. 1. A confidant; one who is entrusted with a secret. 2. One cognizant of, or privy to a criminal transaction; a confidant; an accessory to a fact. rāz-dārī , n. f. 1. Keeping a secret. 2. Having the knowledge of a crime and not giving information. rāz fāsh k. , v. a. To betray a secret; expose one. rāz-i-ma-khfī , A hidden secret; a private matter. rāz o niyāz , 1. Petition; prayer. r: re, ra, rāʼe mǒhmala, rāʼe manqūt̤a, r, ṛ, l, abjad, rabīʻuʼl-awwal, ra-kār
ety: called, in Urdū, rāʼe mǒhmala, and rāʼe manqūt̤a
def: s.m. The letter or sound ra.
more: called rāë mohmala and rāë-gair-manqūta , is the 10th letter in the Arabic, 12th in the Persian, and 14th in the Hindustānī alphabet, corresponding to र ra , the 27th consonant in the Hindi and Sanskrit alphabet. In abjad q. v. it stands for the number 200. In almanacs it stands for the moon, and the month rabi-ul-avval . As an initial, medial, and final it is interchangable with l , as rekh=lekh line; E. rasrī = E. lasrī rope; karejā=kalejā liver; urajhnā=ulajhnā to be entangled; mandar=manḍal temple; dīvār= dīv/?/l wall; dhūr=dhūl dirt: with s as raṅgtarah =saṅgtarah orange; raṛaknā=saraknā to move; nikārnā=nikāsnā drive away. As initial and medial, with k , as rauṅdnā=khūṅdnā trample; rarak=karak prick; dhorā=dhokā cheat: with p , as relnā=pelnā push; dhorā=dhopā cheat. As a medial and final with n , as jaram=janam birth; phir=phin again: with t , as parnālā=patnālā a water spout; jag…

is the tenth letter in the Arabic and twelfth in the Persian alphabet, corresponding to र in Sanskrit. Its pronunciation is that of the English r when fully sounded. In denoting numbers by the letters of the Arabic alphabet, it stands for 200. It is interchangeable with ل and د; as, يدسل for يرسل he will send; دجره for دجله the river Tigris; اروند for الوند the name of a mountain. Sometimes ي is used for it; as, قيراط for قرّاط the name of a weight. In Hindī, besides ल it is also interchanged with ड . In almanacks, it is the astronomical character for the moon: and, in dates, it denotes the month ربيع الاوّل.
